_meta.description: AstroPay Figma component node IDs — live-extracted from Figma source
_meta.sync: Manual — re-extract via Figma MCP after every design system publish
_meta.sync_note: 2026-05-04 (re-sync) — Email marketing component library reconciled against live Figma. Two changes detected: (1) Promo email content_sizes shrunk ~169px on XXL/XL/L/M/S (XS unchanged) — likely the community-CTA section was removed from the inline footer area; rerun an M template inspection before the next email build to confirm. (2) Two text_layouts removed: Text Layout=Rappi (950:2427) and Text Layout=Uber (950:2435). Library now exposes 6 text layouts instead of 8 — cobrand-specific text compositions consolidated; use single_text or top_left_text paired with the cobrand header variants. 2026-05-04 (initial) — Email marketing component library added (section 950:1972). New top-level key: email_templates. Twelve component groups: promo_email (6 sizes), headers (11 variants), text_layouts, cobranding_logos (22 partners), illustrations (8 use cases — distinct from in-app newsletter_illustration set 789:1786), ctas, menus, onboarding_cta, content_blocks, onboarding_headers, onboarding_horizontal, footers (3 languages × 2 styles).
_meta.runtime_rule: image_node values below are documentation only — at runtime, ALWAYS resolve image slots via findOne(n => n.name === '#Image') after creating or cloning the template instance. Property keys (Title#xxx, Subheadline#xxx, Gradient/Overlay BOOLEAN toggles) vary per format and must be discovered at runtime via Object.keys(inst.componentProperties).

backgrounds._note: Backgrounds section on Design Components page — reference layers for #Image / solid color / image+gradient setups

image_gradient.label: Image + Gradient
image_gradient.use: Image with brand gradient overlay — fullbg ad starting point

brand_assets._note: Brand identity assets (App Icon / ISO / Logotype / Wordmark). Cross-channel — used by paid media (fullbg layouts), in-app (illustration / promo_cobrand), and email (footers, onboarding headers). Never modify fills inside these instances except for fullbg color overrides documented in PAID-MEDIA-AGENTS.md or for white-on-dark fills in email dark-mode footers.

ad_templates._note: Default = Layout=Design 1. Design 4/5 = fullbg (image fills entire frame, no mockup). 16:9 has no Design 5 — variants are 1, 2, 3, 4, 6.

design_2._note: Replaced 2026-04-30 — new node ID. image_node must be discovered at runtime via findOne(n => n.name === '#Image').

design_5._note: Added 2026-04-30. image_node must be discovered at runtime via findOne(n => n.name === '#Image').

inapp.label: InApp
inapp._note: Lifecycle in-app messages — 6 layouts. Use Promo/Cobrand and Cashback Promo for promo flows; Layout6 added 2026-04-30.

newsletter_illustration.label: Newsletters - Illustration (In-App)
newsletter_illustration._note: IN-APP illustration set — 8 use cases. Used by the in-app `illustration` layout. For EMAIL illustrations, use `email_templates.illustrations` (950:2716) — DO NOT cross-reference. Spin variant added 2026-04-30. 'Astronaunt' is intentionally spelled this way in the source file.

email_templates._note: Email marketing component library — added 2026-05-04. Source of truth for ALL email work (lifecycle, promo, transactional, onboarding). NEVER reference the in-app `newsletter_illustration` set (789:1786) for email — use `email_templates.illustrations` (950:2716) instead. All subkeys below are independent component sets that compose into a full email.
_runtime_caveats: Matter font is typically NOT installed in working files. Before any TEXT edit, swap fontName Matter → Inter. Style mapping: Matter SemiBold → Inter 'Semi Bold' (with a space, not 'SemiBold'). Same rule documented in PAID-MEDIA-AGENTS.md § Plugin API rules — applies to email work without modification.

Email header variants (under `headers`) do NOT expose Gradient/Overlay BOOLEAN properties like paid-media fullbg layouts (design_4 / design_5). When using white text over a photographic hero, contrast against busy lifestyle photos is suboptimal — prefer less-busy heroes (e.g. cleaner `traveler` or `places` close-ups, or illustrated heroes from `email_templates.illustrations`) when text legibility matters.

When swapping a header variant on an existing email instance, nested cobrand assets (Logo Section > Logo=<partner> instance) MAY persist visible even after toggling `Cobrand?=false`. To fully remove the cobrand asset for a non-cobrand send: set `node.visible = false` on the nested `Logo Section` INSTANCE inside the header (not the outer Logo Section FRAME).

Promo % header text slots (the big number + small word in `header_cta`, `big_30_off`, `big_30_cb`, `repetition_cb`) are sized for SHORT numbers (e.g. '20%' = 3 chars). For longer overrides like '2,4%' or '12.5%', set `textAutoResize = 'WIDTH_AND_HEIGHT'` on the TEXT node to prevent line wrap.

Promo email frames historically carried an INLINE footer block (3 TEXT nodes: community CTA + disclaimer + links). On 2026-05-04 the templates shrunk ~169px each (XXL/XL/L/M/S — XS unchanged), suggesting the community-CTA inline section was removed and the templates may now compose with the standalone `footers` instances (950:1444 / 950:1470 / 950:1496 / rounded variants). VERIFY structurally on first edit: walk the cloned template's children, check if a footer-like Frame still exists inline, OR if the bottom is empty and an instance of `footers.standard.<lang>` should be appended. Update this caveat after the next M template inspection.

Default header variant of M template (950:2260) is `Header=Rappi` — a cobrand-default. The default menu variant is `Property 1=Menu Logo Dark`. For a non-cobrand BR or AR FX promo, expect to swap BOTH variants (menu → `Menu Logo Light`, header → a non-cobrand variant) before any text edits.

promo_email.label: Newsletters - Promo email
promo_email._note: Full promo email frame — 6 content sizes. Pick by total content density: XS (announcement / single CTA) → XXL (multi-section campaign). Default for cobrand and cashback campaigns is M or L.

xxl.use: Multi-section campaign — hero + 3-4 content blocks + footer. Resized 2026-05-04 (was 3991).

xl.use: Multi-section — hero + 2-3 content blocks + footer. Resized 2026-05-04 (was 3397).

l.use: Standard promo — hero + 2 content blocks + footer. Resized 2026-05-04 (was 2890).

m.use: Single-purpose — hero + 1 content block + footer (default for most promos). Resized 2026-05-04 (was 2296).

s.use: Short promo — hero + CTA + footer. Resized 2026-05-04 (was 1747).

xs.use: Announcement / micro-update — minimal hero + CTA + footer. Unchanged 2026-05-04.

headers.label: Newsletters - Header
headers._note: Email hero/header variants. Cobrand-named headers (Rappi, Uber) are partner-specific — only use with an active cobrand agreement (see @email-legal). Big 30% OFF / Big 30 CB / Repetition CB are promo-percentage headers — promo % must be a real, current value (no placeholders).

header_cta.use: Big number/% + embedded CTA. The variant's NAME suggests a generic header with CTA, but the DEFAULT visual treatment is cobrand-promo (Cobrand?=true, big-percentage + partner logo). For non-cobrand use: (1) toggle `Cobrand?=false` on the header instance, (2) toggle `CTA?=true` to make the embedded CTA visible, (3) hide the nested `Logo Section` INSTANCE (the partner logo placeholder remains visible otherwise). Validated 2026-05-04 by adapting it to FX rate-trigger emails — the big-number slot works well for percentage rate movements (e.g. '2,4%' / 'caiu').

cobrand_bottom.use: Cobrand logo positioned at the bottom of header

1_1.use: Square 1:1 hero — for cropped or social-shared emails

xs.use: Minimal/short header — pair with promo_email content size XS

text_layouts.label: Newsletters - Text Layout
text_layouts._note: Body text composition variants. Cashback / Big 30 CB / Big 30 OFF are promo-specific compositions. `single_text` is the default body layout for non-promo emails. `top_left_text` pairs with right-aligned hero. For cobrand emails (Rappi, Uber, etc.), use `single_text` or `top_left_text` paired with the cobrand header variants under `headers` — cobrand-named text layouts (`Text Layout=Rappi` 950:2427 and `Text Layout=Uber` 950:2435) were removed from the design system on 2026-05-04.

single_text.use: Single column text — default body layout

top_left_text.use: Left-aligned text block — pairs with right-aligned hero

cobranding_logos.label: Newsletters - Cobranding
cobranding_logos._note: Partner brand logos. Listing here = the design system carries the asset; it does NOT mean AstroPay has permission to send a campaign with it. Every cobrand send requires an ACTIVE agreement covering AstroPay's use of the partner's brand assets — confirm via @email-legal before launch. Firing a stale cobrand creates legal exposure.

ctas.label: Newsletters - Call to Action
ctas._note: Email-specific CTA component — alignment variants only. CTA color and shape inherit from `components.cta` (default `teal_rd`).

illustrations.label: Newsletters - Illustration (Email)
illustrations._note: Email-specific illustration set — 8 use cases. Distinct component instances from the in-app `newsletter_illustration` set (789:1786) — DO NOT cross-reference. 'Astronaunt' is intentionally spelled this way in the source file.

menus.label: Newsletters - Menu
menus._note: Top-of-email navigation — 4 styles (dark/light × with/without logo). Pair Dark with promo emails on dark hero variants; Light with onboarding/transactional emails. Logo variant adds the AstroPay wordmark inline.

onboarding_cta.label: Newsletters - Onboarding CTA
onboarding_cta._note: Onboarding email CTA blocks — 3 sizes. Use S for follow-up nudges (KYC reminder), M for default activation, L for hero CTA in welcome emails.

content_blocks.label: Content
content_blocks._note: Modular content blocks for assembling email body sections inside `promo_email` frames. Mix freely.

img_h1_body_cta.use: Image hero + headline + body + CTA — image leads

h1_img_copy_cta.use: Headline + image + body + CTA — headline leads

onboarding_headers.label: Onboarding - header
onboarding_headers._note: Onboarding email header layouts — 3 layouts (all named 'Layout=1' in the source — distinguish by node ID).

footers._note: Mandatory footer at the bottom of every marketing and lifecycle email. 'rounded' variants have rounded top corners — pair with rounded-corner hero. Match language to audience: `eng` default, `esp` for AR market (ES-AR), `pr` for BR market (PT-BR). The footer carries the unsubscribe link and FSA1399 disclaimer placeholder — never rebuild manually, always swap by language.
